Achievements

Roster
Meet the Team

Three students from Frisco, TX competing at the highest levels of VEX IQ robotics.

01 — Builder
Charlie
Builder

6th grade · Cobb Middle School. Loves building VEX, rock climbing, and tennis. Second year with the Ninjas.

02 — Driver & Coder
Luke
DriverCoder

6th grade · Robinson Middle School. Handles robot programming and driving. Hobbies include VEX, tennis, and guitar.

03 — Driver
Preston
Driver

6th grade · Vandeventer Middle School. Passionate about robotics, Lego, soccer, and puzzles.

Community
Outreach & Service

Beyond competing, the Robotex Ninjas bring STEM education and robotics to kids and families across Frisco. Tap any event to expand.

01
Robotics for Kids at Local Church
Workshop  •  Frisco, TX
Workshop⌄

Organized and led hands-on VEX IQ workshops for young kids at a local church, introducing building and programming in a fun, accessible setting. Kids engaged with robots, asked great questions, and got their first taste of what competitive robotics looks like.

1 / 1
02
Volunteer at Magikids Events
Volunteering  •  Frisco, TX
Volunteering⌄

Served as event volunteers at Magikids robotics competitions, helping run matches, assist younger teams, and support the VEX IQ community in the Frisco area. Photos coming soon.

03
Feed the Hunger
Community Service  •  Frisco, TX
Community Service⌄

Participated in Feed the Hunger food packing events, contributing to hunger relief efforts in the local community alongside fellow volunteers. Photos coming soon.

04
2025–2026 Mix & Match Scrimmage
Event Organizer  •  April 12, 2026
Event Organizer⌄

Team 276C organized a full Mix & Match scrimmage event on April 12, 2026, bringing together 6 teams to compete, practice, and celebrate the season. Plano West FTC Robotics joined as special guests to share their journey — how they chose FTC, what the high school team pathway looks like, and what it takes to compete at the next level. Kids got hands-on field time, ran real matches, and finished with a team party.

1 / 13
05
Public Robotics Demo at Local Library
Public Event  •  Frisco, TX
Public Event⌄

Held a public robotics demonstration at the local library, showcasing VEX IQ robots to families and inspiring the next generation of engineers in the Frisco community. Photos coming soon.

Log
Team Updates

A look back at the milestones that defined our journey. Tap any update to expand.

🌎 World Championship
May 12–14, 2025  •  Dallas, TX
Division 3rd Place at the World Championship
⌄

Team 276C represented Texas Region 5 at the VEX Robotics World Championship in Dallas, competing against the top VEX IQ teams from around the globe. Over three intense days, the Ninjas navigated qualifying rounds, alliance selections, and elimination matches before finishing 3rd in their division — an extraordinary achievement on the world stage. The team brought home their trophy, made new friends from across the world, and celebrated with a well-deserved sushi dinner.

1 / 10
🏆 Regional Champions
2025–2026 Season  •  Mix & Match
Back-to-Back Champions, Think Award & Worlds Qualifier
⌄

A season defined by resilience. After a tough start, the Robotex Ninjas fought back consistently — match by match — to claim the Teamwork Champion, Skills Champion, and Think Award at the Texas Region 5 Championship. The results qualified the team for Worlds for the third consecutive time.

They finished the regular season ranked #94 in the world, #38 in the United States, and #2 in Texas — their strongest global standing to date.

1 / 2
🧠 Think Award
2024–2025 Season
First Think Award in Rapid Relay
⌄

Alongside two championship titles, the team earned the Think Award recognizing the depth and quality of their engineering design process. Photos coming soon.

⭐ Excellence Award
2023–2024 Season
Region 5 Excellence Award
⌄

In their debut Full Volume season, the Robotex Ninjas earned the highest all-around VEX IQ honor — the Excellence Award. Photos coming soon.
